items.context: This statement is designed to measure support for traditional gender role norms in labor market allocation, a common indicator of gender conservatism in comparative political behavior research.
This statement captures attitudes toward in-group economic prioritization and immigration restriction, often used in political science to assess welfare chauvinism and national preference.
This statement measures beliefs about gender hierarchy within the household, commonly used to assess adherence to traditional family structures and patriarchal norms.
This statement assesses norms of intergenerational obligation, frequently used in cross-cultural research on familialism and welfare attitudes.
This statement captures internalized family duty and filial piety, concepts often used in political culture research to measure collectivist versus individualist orientations.
This statement measures attitudes toward maternal employment and its perceived social consequences, a standard indicator in gender role and family policy research.
This statement assesses gender bias in political leadership, commonly used to evaluate support for gender equality in political representation.
This statement measures gender inequality in educational investment, often used as an indicator of traditionalism in human capital attitudes.
This statement captures gender stereotyping in economic leadership roles, frequently used in studies of workplace inequality and gender norms.
This statement assesses normative beliefs about the value of domestic versus paid labor, commonly used to evaluate traditional family role orientations.
This statement measures preferences for income redistribution versus merit-based inequality, a core dimension in political economy and welfare state research.
This statement captures attitudes toward state versus private ownership, a foundational indicator of economic ideology in comparative political systems.
This statement assesses beliefs about the role of government versus individual responsibility in welfare provision, widely used in studies of redistribution preferences.
This statement measures normative evaluations of market competition, a key attitudinal component in pro-market versus interventionist ideology.
This statement captures beliefs about the determinants of economic success (effort versus structural factors), commonly used in research on meritocracy and inequality.
This statement assesses trade-offs between environmental protection and economic growth, a central dimension in political economy and environmental politics.
This statement measures tolerance of welfare fraud, commonly used as an indicator of civic morality and compliance with state institutions.
This statement captures everyday rule-breaking behavior, often used to assess norms of civic honesty and public compliance.
This statement measures attitudes toward property rights violations, a baseline indicator of moral restrictiveness.
This statement assesses tolerance of tax evasion, frequently used in studies of institutional trust and fiscal compliance.
This statement captures attitudes toward corruption, a key variable in governance and anti-corruption research.
This statement measures acceptance of homosexuality, widely used as an indicator of cultural liberalism and social tolerance.
This statement assesses moral attitudes toward abortion, a core issue in studies of moral traditionalism.
This statement captures acceptance of divorce, commonly used to measure changing family norms.
This statement measures permissiveness regarding premarital sex, a standard indicator of sexual liberalization.
This statement assesses moral views on suicide, often used in broader measures of life ethics and moral permissiveness.
This statement captures attitudes toward euthanasia, a key issue in bioethics and moral policy research.
This statement measures tolerance of domestic violence, an indicator of acceptance of interpersonal violence.
This statement assesses attitudes toward corporal punishment of children, often used in studies of authority and violence norms.
This statement captures general tolerance of interpersonal violence, a core dimension of moral restrictiveness.
This statement measures acceptance of political violence, frequently used in studies of extremism and democratic stability.
This statement assesses support for capital punishment, a key indicator in research on punitive attitudes and state authority.
This statement measures perceived procedural fairness in elections, a central component of electoral integrity.
This statement captures perceptions of candidate exclusion, commonly used to assess electoral competitiveness.
This statement measures perceived vote buying, a standard indicator of electoral corruption.
This statement assesses perceptions of media neutrality, an important factor in democratic election quality.
This statement captures trust in election administration, a key dimension of institutional legitimacy.
This statement measures perceptions of elite capture of elections, often used in studies of inequality and political influence.
This statement assesses perceptions of voter intimidation, a core indicator of coercion in elections.
This statement captures perceptions of genuine electoral choice, a defining feature of democratic competition.
This statement measures support for media deference to authority in crisis situations, commonly used as an indicator of authoritarian attitudes.
This statement assesses willingness to trade civil liberties for security, a core dimension of authoritarianism.
This statement captures support for executive dominance over legislative institutions, a key feature of authoritarian governance preferences.
This statement measures preference for leader-centric decision-making over participatory processes.
This statement assesses support for evidence-based policymaking, commonly used to measure technocratic orientation.
This statement captures distrust of experts relative to ordinary citizens, often used to assess anti-technocratic or populist tendencies.
This statement measures preference for direct popular decision-making, a core component of populist and majoritarian democratic attitudes.
This statement captures anti-elite sentiment, a defining characteristic of populist ideology.
This statement measures perception of society as divided between elites and ordinary people, a central element of populist worldviews.
This statement measures support for redistributive economic policies as a component of democratic governance.
This statement captures support for religious authority in lawmaking, often used to assess departures from secular liberal democracy.
This statement measures support for free and fair elections, a core institutional feature of liberal democracy.
This statement assesses support for social welfare provisions within democratic systems.
This statement captures acceptance of military intervention in governance, a key indicator of authoritarian democratic preferences.
This statement measures support for civil rights protections, a foundational element of liberal democracy.
This statement assesses support for economic equality as a democratic goal.
This statement captures preference for obedience to authority over democratic contestation.
This statement measures support for gender equality as a component of democratic values.
